resources.string: Speed, power and gear can\'t update after an app update mid-drive. Turn the car off and on once to restore them.

The car has been off for %1$s min, so this trip would normally stop now and the live range projection would reset. Keep recording if you\'re staying with the car (e.g. parked on a call), or stop the trip.
AWD drivetrain — tap to change pressure unit
Change drive mode to initialize display
Screenshots are a Pro feature — unlock Pro in Settings.
Auto detection will start a trip when you drive
Press Record to begin manual tracking
The current trip will be saved and closed. This cannot be undone.
With manual tracking enabled, trip data will not be recorded automatically when you start driving. You will need to start and stop each trip yourself.
Trips that are not recorded will be absent from your daily, weekly, and monthly consumption statistics.
Recorded telemetry samples plus the current live reading
12V / SOC chart (If offline mode, values are reconstructed)
Start driving to record your first trip!
Tap the ☆ on a trip to mark it a favourite
Try adjusting or clearing the filters
Touch trip for analytics, long-press for multiple selection
This will permanently delete the selected trips and all their data. This action cannot be undone.
The two selected trips will be combined into one. Distance, energy and driving time are added together; the start of the earlier trip and the end of the later trip are kept. This cannot be undone.
12 V system, HVAC, and model residual. Shown as remainder after rolling, aero, and gradient. Motor/inverter losses are factored into the figures above
Save as HTML viewer (double-click to open)
Set up the bot in Settings → Backup & Restore.
Enter the total amount you paid for DC charging during this trip. The app will deduct the overlapping charging energy from the fixed home-tariff estimate and add this custom cost instead.
Tap the ☆ on a session to mark it a favourite
Sessions are reconstructed automatically\nfrom SoC changes on each car wake-up
This will permanently delete the selected charging sessions and all their data. This action cannot be undone.
The live chart will appear as soon as enough charging-power samples are recorded.
This session was recorded while the car was off — live telemetry charts are only available for sessions started while you are in the car with it powered on.
Complete at least 2 trips with telemetry active to see battery degradation tracking.
%1$s earlier trip(s) are hidden because they were recorded with a legacy method that under-reported SoH. You can include them, though the chart may show a misleading dip.

SoH (State of Health) is shown when the car exposes a direct battery-health value, or as an estimate derived from vehicle telemetry when a direct value is unavailable. 100% means the pack is at factory capacity. The orange trend line is a least-squares linear fit across all your recorded trips.
Tip: BYD\'s warranty covers the battery to 70% SoH for 8 years / 250,000 km.

Export a report of your battery\'s State of Health, decline rate and 80% projection — useful evidence when selling the car or raising a warranty claim. Saved to Download/BydTripStats/ as a print-ready PDF or as HTML (opens in any browser). Generated entirely on-device.
Hiding %1$s early trip(s) recorded before %2$s with the legacy estimation method (recommended).
Using every recorded trip — none predate the statistical SoH method.
Including every recorded trip, even early ones whose calculated SoH may read low.
This only changes what the chart and reports use — no trips are ever deleted, and you can change it back anytime.

This re-includes early trips recorded with the legacy method, which under-estimated SoH — it will add a misleading dip to the chart and to any report you generate. Nothing is deleted; you can switch back anytime.
Complete trips across multiple seasons to see how temperature affects your range.

Your winter consumption is %1$s %2$s higher than summer (+%3$s%%). This is primarily battery chemistry — lithium cells deliver less capacity below 15°C and the BMS draws extra power to maintain pack temperature. Cabin heating compounds the effect.
Drive the same journey at least 3 times — like a daily commute — and it\'ll appear here so you can compare efficiency across each trip.
Tap ✏️ above to set a consumption or distance target.
Leave a field blank to disable that goal.
Avg of last 5 trips must be ≤ this value
Add a tag to a trip from its detail screen, or tag several at once from History\'s selection mode. Tagged trips are summarised here.
This removes the tag from %1$s trip(s). The trips themselves are kept.
Tap a tag to apply it to the selected trips:
No tags yet — create one below.
Saves the full trip database to: Download/BydTripStats/byd_stats_backup_DATE.db
Saves a copy of the database to a BydTripStats folder on the SD card. Unlike the Download backup, these survive an app uninstall — off-device redundancy you can pull out and read on a computer.
This is a BYD Trip Stats Pro feature.
No SD card detected. Insert a card, then reopen this screen.
Restoring will replace ALL current trip data. The app will close and reopen automatically.
No backups found. Run a backup first.
Removes diagnostic data and downsamples old recordings to free up space:

WARNING: Downsampling means that charts/heatmaps from trimmed sessions will have very scarce points thus not be in detail.\nIf you just want to reduce storage usage, you might want to delete older trips instead of trimming.\nTHIS ACTION IS IRREVERSIBLE!
Trim complete — app will restart in a few seconds to finalise space reclaim.
Trim complete. (VACUUM was skipped; restart the app later to reclaim space.)
This will permanently delete all trips and statistics.\n\nA backup will be saved to Download automatically before the reset. The app will close and reopen.
This will permanently replace ALL current trip data with %1$s.\n\nThe app will close and reopen automatically after restore.
Schedule updated to %1$s. Next auto-backup will follow the new interval.
Once connected, backups can be sent manually or on a schedule. Each backup lands as a .db file in your private chat, accessible from any device with Telegram.
1. Message @BotFather on Telegram → /newbot → copy the token\n2. Send any message to your new bot\n3. Paste the token below and tap Validate
Connect a Telegram private bot first (see Telegram Backup above).
Tap refresh to scan your Telegram chat for .db backups.
Telegram not configured — set it up under Backup & Restore first.
What your drive and regen modes actually did on this trip
Value above WLTP average — capped at rated range
Not enough driving data for instantaneous consumption
Very short axle bursts may appear smoothed - fullscreen for details
Motor output at each speed — shows where the car actually operates
Instantaneous efficiency (%1$s) across the speed range
Regenerative braking strength by speed band
Front motor operating map — near-linear for a direct-drive EV
Rear motor operating map — near-linear for a direct-drive EV
Dominant motor RPM operating map — near-linear for a direct-drive EV
Thermal operating window — higher power available as pack warms up
Where the driver accelerates hard or coasts — lower is more efficient
Whether efficiency changes at low or high charge states
When and how fast you drive — reveals rush-hour congestion patterns
How slope affects energy — regen visible in negative-gradient band
AWD torque split — diagonal = equal share, off-diagonal = one motor dominant
Whether higher pressure measurably improves efficiency on your car
Where BMS throttles regenerative braking — expected near 100% SoC
Motorway thermal load vs stop-start urban — cleaner than Power vs Temp
Flat = healthy pack · Divergence spike at low SoC = weak cell
Not enough data points for heatmaps.
Not enough altitude/distance data for this heatmap.
No dual-motor RPM data recorded on this trip.
No regenerative braking data recorded on this trip.
No cell voltage data recorded on this trip.
Not enough driving samples for this heatmap.
Not enough speed transitions for this heatmap.
Not enough data for time-of-day heatmap.
No battery temperature data recorded on this trip.
No %1$s mode recorded — on future trips tap each mode on the car display once to enable analytics.
No tyre pressure data recorded on this trip
Choose the BYD model you drive
This selection will be saved and used to load the correct car configuration.
Only DiLink 3 vehicles are supported. DiLink 4 and 5 are not yet supported.
Choose how the app looks. System default follows your device\'s dark/light setting.
Choose the app display language. Selecting a language overrides the device system language for this app.
Choose how distances and speeds are displayed. Your vehicle\'s odometer and speed values come from the BMS and are already in the correct unit for your market.

When enabled, the range-projection card shows a liquid-fill battery icon, an AWD/axle drawing with tyre pressure/temperature overlays, and an animated consumption thumbnail above the chart. When disabled, those move into the top bar (battery and consumption icons) and a dedicated Tyres stat card on the side panel — freeing vertical space for the range chart and skipping all animations.

How long the trip stays open after the car turns off. If the car comes back on within this window the recording resumes seamlessly (same trip, a new segment appears along with the cumulative distance in parenthesis). Past the window the trip ends and the next drive starts a new one. Default is 3 minutes.

When the timeout is reached while the app is open on screen, show a prompt to keep the trip going (and its live range projection) instead of stopping it — handy when you park but stay with the car. If you don\'t have the app open, or you leave it, the trip still stops automatically as usual. A kept trip ends once you drive again, tap Stop, or after ~30 min parked.

Auto-discard trips shorter than this when they end (datapoints, segments and stats are removed too). Useful for filtering out moving the car a few meters in the driveway or very short distances. Set to 0 to disable.

Heads-up: discarded trips disappear from everything that reads the trip table — history list, weekly/monthly/yearly consumption charts, monthly distance and energy totals, seasonal analysis, and the SoH degradation series. A high threshold over a quiet day means no point will be plotted for that day. The chart already ignores trips under 0.5 km, so only thresholds above that change the chart further.
Choose which battery percentage reading to display on the dashboard.

On PHEVs the BMS SoC is usually not reported — use Panel if BMS shows 0.\nBMS is more accurate (float) than Panel (integer). Also, larger divergence from Panel is a great indication that it is time for either 100% charge or charging calibration
Notify me when the pack\'s cell voltage spread stays above the limit.

Spread = highest cell − lowest cell. A healthy pack stays under ~20 mV; a persistently high spread can flag a weak cell. Alerts are suppressed below 5%% and above 95%% SoC, where a wide spread is normal.
This is a BYD Trip Stats Pro feature. Unlock Pro to receive imbalance alerts.
Set your home charging tariff so trip costs can be estimated consistently.
Background activity when car is off
Controls whether the telemetry service keeps running after the car is parked.

Always On (recommended default): service runs 24/7, taking continuous 12V/SoC samples into battery history and capturing off-state (e.g. overnight) charging. Small additional load on top of BYD's own stock background drain.

Note: most BYD units cut WiFi ~15 min after the car is switched off, and the app can't override that. So while parked, ADB-over-WiFi, the Web Companion (PWA) and MQTT to a broker on your home network stop until the car powers on. MQTT to an internet-reachable broker (e.g. HiveMQ Cloud) usually keeps publishing while parked, because the car stays on mobile data.
Note2: Electro app can override the above and keep the WiFi indefinitely when car is off

Minimal: service self-stops 5 min after the car turns off, then a 90-min alarm briefly wakes it for a charging snapshot. Lower drain, but off-state samples are sparse, ADB is not always on, the Web Companion (PWA) is only reachable during the brief wake blip, and MQTT is idle between blips. Off-state charging is caught within ~90 min, after which it publishes for the rest of the charge.

Deep Sleep: service self-stops 5 min after the car turns off with no further wakeups. Allows the car\'s ECUs to reach full deep sleep. The Web Companion (PWA) is unreachable and MQTT is silent the whole time the car is parked; off-state charging is not captured live — only reconstructed from the SoC delta when the car next turns on.

Keepalive: never fired yet%1$s. If this stays \"never\" after the car has been parked a while, your head unit powers off fully at park — Minimal then behaves like Deep Sleep, so use Always On for parked data.
Enter your home charging price so trip costs can use your fixed tariff as the baseline.
Trip stays open for this many minutes after the engine turns off. Default is %1$s min.
Trips shorter than this are discarded when they end. Set to 0 (or leave empty) to keep every trip.
Alert when the cell voltage spread stays above this for a few seconds. Typical limit: 50 mV. Allowed range: 10–500 mV.
Active — Pro unlocked for this vehicle (lifetime).

This turns Pro off on this vehicle and disables the Pro features (cell imbalance alert, battery health report, screenshots, SD card backup). Your code isn\'t lost — you can re-enter it anytime to unlock again.
Unlock premium features like the battery cell imbalance alert and dashboard screenshots with a one-time code for your vehicle — €9.99, lifetime, one car. Verified on-device — nothing leaves your car.
This vehicle\'s ID (send this when buying a licence):
Reading… start the car, then reopen Settings
Scan this with your phone to email your Vehicle ID — just choose your email account and press Send.
Goes to %1$s — you\'ll get a reply with payment steps (€9.99) and your unlock code.
Enter the unlock code you received after purchase. It\'s a short, vehicle-specific code, checked on-device — nothing leaves your car.
That code isn\'t valid for this vehicle.
Start the car so the app can read your Vehicle ID, then try again.
Pro verification is unavailable in this build.
A saved code doesn\'t match this vehicle.
Browse trips & charging from any device on the same WiFi.
Start a local server so you can open trip history in a browser.

Served over your local WiFi, so it\'s reachable while the car is on and the telemetry service is alive. Once parked, most BYD units cut WiFi ~15 min after the car is off — after that the companion is unreachable (there\'s no mobile-data fallback for a local server) until the car powers on again, in every Background activity mode.\nNote: Electro app has an option to keep WiFi alive indefinitely
Try 8081, 8888, 9090 if a port is blocked. Changes restart the server.
Shown on the browser login page when someone opens the server URL.
Min 4 digits. Changing the PIN invalidates all active browser sessions.
Too many incorrect PIN attempts detected.
Open this address on any device connected to the same WiFi.
Choose a connection to configure. The cards below show live status at a glance.
A Better Routeplanner uploads live telemetry.
Publish live telemetry to a broker such as HiveMQ or HomeAssistant.
Link Generic token uploads live telemetry to A Better Routeplanner.
Get your ABRP Link Generic token from ABRP web or app. \nGo to Settings → Vehicle → Live data → Edit connections → In-car live data → Link Generic
Turn on to configure ABRP uploads.
Telemetry not ready yet - wait a second and try again
Turn on to configure MQTT publishing.
Publishes the live telemetry JSON every %1$s s.
Port 8883 normally requires TLS — without it the connection is unencrypted and will likely fail.
MQTT over WebSocket so the broker can sit behind an HTTP reverse proxy.
Driving: every %1$s s · Charging / idle while running: every 30 s

While parked, publishing depends on the Background activity mode and your broker. In Always On the service keeps running, but most BYD units cut WiFi ~15 min after the car is off — so a broker on your home network stops receiving until the car powers on, while an internet-reachable broker (e.g. HiveMQ Cloud) usually keeps getting data over mobile data. Minimal publishes only during the ~90-min wake blips, and Deep Sleep not at all.
⚠️ ADB setup required — open app to trigger setup dialog
Install button will become available when parked with no active trip or charging
Contrary to older versions, is it true that I no longer need the Electro app?

Correct. BYD Trip Stats works as a standalone app on supported vehicles, so trip recording and charging sessions work without Electro.\n\nElectro is still a useful companion app for owners who want its own features, but it is no longer required for BYD Trip Stats to function.
Do I need internet or mobile signal?

No for normal use. Trips, charging sessions, charts, and local history are recorded entirely on-device.\n\nInternet is only needed for optional features such as app update checks, opening web links, MQTT / ABRP integration and Telegram backups if you enable them.
What is BYD Trip Stats Pro and what does it include?

Pro is an optional one-time unlock for premium extras — a small thank-you that helps support development. It's €9.99, paid once, for the lifetime of one vehicle (no subscription). Everything that was free stays free; Pro only adds new features:

• Cell imbalance alert — get notified when the pack's cell-voltage spread stays above a limit
• Battery health report — export a printable PDF/HTML of your State of Health, decline rate and projected date to 80%%
• Dashboard screenshots — tap the BYD logo to save the current screen
• SD card backup — save the database to a BydTripStats folder on a removable SD card

Unlocking: the code is tied to your specific car. Open Settings → Preferences → BYD Trip Stats Pro, scan the QR code with your phone (or copy your Vehicle ID) to request a code, then paste it back in. It's verified entirely on-device — no account, no sign-in, nothing leaves your car.
Why is \'auto/unknown\' shown at driving / regen modes?

The app reads drive and regen modes directly from the car's instrument cluster, but the car only broadcasts a mode value when you actively change it — it does not report the current mode on startup.

Until you tap a mode on the car display, the app has no value to show and falls back to 'Auto' (regen) or 'Unknown' (drive). To fix this:

1. Cycle through each regen level on the car's touchscreen once
2. Cycle through each drive mode (Eco / Normal / Sport) once

The app will immediately pick up both values and start recording them with every trip data point. You only need to do this once.
No network connectivity means trip will be missing from the history?

No. Trip history is stored locally and does not depend on mobile signal or Wi-Fi.\n\nA trip may close a little later only if live telemetry itself stops for a while. In that case the watchdog closes the active trip after 3 minutes of telemetry silence, anchored to the last packet the app received.
The app stops working after a car restart

You need to exclude BYD Trip Stats from the autostart-killer:

1. Open the Disable Autostart app (native BYD app, usually near the file explorer)
2. Find BYD Trip Stats and toggle its entry OFF — same as you have done for Electro
3. Open the app and hold the volume button for ~10 seconds to restart the car UI
4. Reopen the app

Important: you must repeat this step after every app update, because the permission resets on reinstall.
How do I back up my trip data?

Several methods are available — all produce the same standard SQLite file:

Local (simplest): Settings → Data → Open Backup & Restore → Backup Now.

SD card (Pro): Backup & Restore → Backup to SD card. Survives an app uninstall.

Telegram (recommended): Connect a bot via Backup & Restore → Telegram Backup, then send manually or on a schedule.

ADB: adb shell run-as com.byd.tripstats cat files/db_backup/FILENAME.db > local.db
I reinstalled the app — can I recover my backups?

Yes. Telegram: reconnect your bot token, then Restore from Telegram → refresh icon rediscovers all backups automatically.\n\nLocal: The .db files in Download/BydTripStats/ survive a reinstall. Open Backup & Restore, tap the refresh icon under Local Restore and they reappear.
How does Telegram automatic backup work?

Enable the Auto-backup toggle and pick Daily, Weekly, or Monthly.

• The first automatic backup runs after one full interval
• If the car is off when due, it fires on next boot
• Failed attempts are retried with exponential backoff

To stop: tap Disconnect bot.
How do I restore a backup pushed from my PC via ADB?

1. adb push backup.db /sdcard/Download/BydTripStats/backup.db
2. Open the app → Backup & Restore → Local Restore
3. Tap the refresh icon — the pushed file appears
4. Tap Restore and confirm

No meaningful impact. In an EV the 12V battery is continuously trickle-charged from the high-voltage pack. BYD Trip Stats only keeps a lightweight foreground service alive to read telemetry and write local trip data.
Can I export data to Excel?

Yes. Open any trip, tap the share icon (top-right), then Save as CSV. The file lands in the Download folder and can be opened directly in Excel, Google Sheets, or any data tool. A JSON export is also available from the same menu.

Completely. BYD Trip Stats collects zero information about you. Everything stays on your device: trip data, GPS coordinates, backups, and settings. The only optional external traffic is what you explicitly trigger.
Where can I get help, request a feature or report a bug?
Check the README.md file at my Github, open an issue there or visit the discord server — tap the link below.\n\nDon\'t forget to include your BYD model and the steps to reproduce the problem.
Auto-refreshes while the App tab is open.
Waiting for live telemetry and vehicle snapshot.
Live process, memory, frame, permission, and ADB tools are visible.
Off by default so this screen does not keep polling while you are just checking backups.
Sends the full diagnostics log straight to your connected Telegram bot — nothing is saved or shared locally. Connect a bot under Backup & Restore → Telegram Backup first.
Enable this only when troubleshooting; the history charts sample every 2 seconds while visible.
⚠️ Background permissions not granted. Open the app fresh to trigger the ADB setup dialog
Runs through the authorized local ADB daemon. Enter the command exactly as you would after `adb shell`.
Samples refresh every 2 seconds while this screen is open.
Help support for your BYD model by sharing a raw telemetry probe report. Enable probing, drive for a couple of minutes, then export and share the file.

The report captures raw values from every data source on your car's head unit — numeric values, strings, and arrays.

GPS latitude and longitude are NOT included.

Everything else is included: battery state, motor data, gear, speed, drive modes, climate state, charging state, PHEV-specific values, etc.

Nothing is sent automatically. "Email via QR code" uploads the report to a temporary public host and shows a link that auto-deletes after 12 hours.
Off — enable, drive briefly, then export.
Scan with your phone — it opens a pre-filled email to %1$s. Just choose your account and press Send. The link expires in 12 hours.
Couldn\'t render the QR code. Use "Copy link" and email it to %1$s.

A new version was installed. You need to toggle-off disable autostart for this app, which enables background data collection when the car is off (e.g. charging overnight).

For best reliability, reboot the car once after changing this setting, then open BYD Trip Stats again.
WARNING: In case you don't see speed and other values updating while driving, you need to reboot the car!

BYD Trip Stats needs one-time ADB authorization to run in the background.

Steps:
1. Settings → Developer Options
2. Enable USB Debugging
3. Return here and tap Authorize
A dialog should appear on screen asking to allow USB debugging. Tap Allow, then return here.\n\nThis is a one-time action. Future updates are fully automatic.
